segments.s136-g25: This clause begins with the prepositional phrase 'به گیتی دران' (literally 'to/into that world'), functioning adverbially 'in this world'. 'کوش' is an exhortative/imperfect-imperative-form verb ('strive' / 'make an effort') addressed to the listener. 'چون بگذری' is a subordinate temporal clause ('when/as you pass') with بگذری in a present/subjunctive form used poetically to mean 'you pass through' or 'you go by'. Word order: [prepositional phrase] + [verb exhortation] + [temporal subordinate].
segments.s136-g26: 'سرانجام' is an adverb meaning 'in the end / finally' that introduces the result. 'نیکی' is the thing gained ('goodness' / 'a good deed'). 'بر خود' is a prepositional phrase meaning 'upon yourself' or 'for yourself' showing the beneficiary. 'بری' here is an archaic/polite-present verbal form meaning 'you will carry/bring/take' (2nd person singular); its exact lexical senses in the supplied list do not match, so treat it as the verb 'to bring/take' used prophetically. The clause therefore means 'in the end you shall bring goodness upon yourself.'
segments.s136-g27: 'کنون' is a temporal adverb 'now'. 'رزم سهراب' is a noun phrase 'the battle of Sohrab' (noun + name of the participant). 'رانم' is a first-person verb form (I ...), here used to mean 'I set forth / I relate / I recount'—an older poetic use of راندن/راندن to introduce a narrative. 'نخست' (first) modifies the verb, marking this as the first account given. Word order: [temporal adverb] + [noun phrase] + [verb (1st sg)] + [adverb].
segments.s136-g28: 'ازان کین' = 'of that enmity / from that quarrel' where 'ازان' ('from that / concerning that') links the topic to what follows. 'که' introduces a relative/subordinate clause ('which/that'), and the clause 'که او با پدر چون بجست' explains the nature of that enmity: 'he, with (his) father, when he strove/met/contended.' 'بجست' is an older past/simple perfect form (3rd singular) from جستن meaning here 'sought/contended/engaged' (poetically 'fought with'). Overall it specifies 'that grudge by which he contended with his father.'
segments.s136-g9: همه تا = 'until all/everyone': تا is the temporal conjunction 'until' and همه is the quantified subject. در آز رفته فراز = a compact participial phrase: در (here = 'gate/door') + آز ('greed') forms a genitive 'gate of greed'; رفته is the past participle 'having gone' placed before the adverb فراز ('up/on high'), so رفته فراز together mean 'having gone up/ascended'.
segments.s136-g10: This clause is inverted poetic word order for 'این در راز باز به کس برنشد' — literally 'this secret door did not open to anyone.' به کس = 'to/for anyone' (به before کس marks the indirect object). بر نشد is a poetic negative past of شدن used with باز ('open') to mean 'did not open'; این در راز = 'this secret door' (این + در 'door' + راز 'secret').
segments.s136-g11: برفتن is the infinitive 'to go away/to depart' used as the verbal noun/topic. مگر here functions as a corrective/softening particle ('rather/perhaps'). بهتر آیدش جای is a reversed word order: جای ('place') qualified by بهتر ('better') with the verb آید (آیدش = 'will come to him' or 'he will get') — literally 'a better place will come to him'.
segments.s136-g12: چو = 'when'. آرام یابد = 'he finds/attains peace' — یابد is third-person present/subjunctive of یابیدن used to mean 'find/attain'. به دیگر سرای = prepositional phrase 'to another house/abode' (به + دیگر + سرای); note سرای here means 'house/abode' but the provided lemma senses do not list that meaning.
segments.s136-g13: دم مرگ is a genitive/construct: 'the moment (breath) of death'. چون introduces a simile 'like/as'. آتش هولناک is the noun + adjective 'a terrifying fire' (adjective follows noun, typical in Persian).
segments.s136-g14: ندارد ... باک = 'has no fear' (ندارد = 'does not have'). ز = poetic form of از meaning 'of/regarding'; ز برنا و فرتوت = 'of (the) young and the decrepit' (two coordinated nouns). The whole means 'it (death's moment) has no dread of the young or the old' — i.e. death spares neither.
segments.s136-g15: درین = contraction of در این 'in this'. جای رفتن = 'place for going' or 'the time/occasion to go' (جای + infinitive). نه جای درنگ = 'there is no place for delay' — نه negates جای درنگ. The clause is a terse, idiomatic prohibition: here one must go; there is no tarrying.
segments.s136-g16: This is inverted poetry: بر اسپ = 'upon the steed' (prepositional phrase). فناگر is an epithet 'destroyer/annihilator' qualifying مرگ (Death). کشد is the present/past-like verb 'pulls/draws/seizes' (verb appears before its subject here by poetic inversion); تنگ modifies کشد as 'tightly/close'. Together: 'Death, the destroyer, pulls tight upon the steed.' Note: the provided sense list for کشد does not include 'pull/seize', so that lemma is not matched.
segments.s136-g1: اگر introduces a conditional clause ('if'). تندبادی is the subject in the indefinite form (an i-suffix noun meaning 'a gale'). براید is an archaic/poetic present subjunctive/indicative meaning 'comes forth' or 'arises' (here 'to come'); ز + کنج is a prepositional phrase 'from (the) nook/corner' following the verb.
segments.s136-g2: بخاک افگند is a verbal phrase: بخاک ('to the ground/onto the earth') is the directional complement and افگند is the past tense 'threw/cast' (poetic past). نارسیده modifies ترنج and is a prefixed adjective 'unripe/premature', so ترنج is the object 'bitter-orange/citron'. The sequence is verb after directional phrase, typical of classical Persian poetic word order.
segments.s136-g3: ستمکاره خوانیمش is a declarative clause: ستمکاره ('oppressor') is a predicate noun and خوانیمش ('we call/read him') is the verb with enclitic object -مش. The following ار is the conditional particle 'if' introducing an elliptical clause (the copula 'باشد' is omitted), so دادگر is the conditional predicate 'just' linked to that omitted verb.
segments.s136-g4: هنرمند دانیمش is parallel in form to the previous clause: هنرمند ('skillful/artful person') is the predicate and دانیمش ('we consider/deem him') is the verb with enclitic object. The following ار again introduces a conditional 'if', here applying to the adjective بی‌هنر ('unskilled'), with an implied copula omitted.
segments.s136-g5: اگر introduces the conditional. مرگ is the subject 'death'. دادست is a poetic fused form ('داد' + 'ست') meaning 'is justice' (ست is the copula). بیداد چیست is a short interrogative clause: بیداد 'injustice' + چیست 'what is (it)?' — here the copula/verb is used after the interrogative.
segments.s136-g6: ز داد is a prepositional phrase 'from/because of justice'. این همه بانگ و فریاد is a noun phrase 'all these cries and laments' (این همه = 'all this/so much', بانگ و فریاد = 'shout and cry'). چیست closes the question 'what is (it)?' — the verb/copula follows the questioned phrase, a normal word order for rhetorical questions in classical Persian.
segments.s136-g7: ازین is the fused prepositional phrase 'of/from this' (از + این in archaic spelling). راز is the noun 'secret' governed by ازین. جان تو is the possessor phrase 'your soul' (noun before the enclitic pronoun تو). آگاه نیست is the negated copula 'is not aware' (آگاه = aware; نیست = is not). Word order places the circumstantial 'ازین راز' first for emphasis.
segments.s136-g8: بدین پرده اندر ترا راه نیست is an idiomatic locative clause: بدین ('to/through this' = ب + این in archaic orthography) modifies پرده ('veil/curtain'). اندر is the old preposition 'in/within' marking location, ترا is the object pronoun 'for you' (ت + را), and راه نیست is the predicate 'there is no way' (راه = way; نیست = is not). The sequence states that passage through this veil is not open to you.
segments.s136-g17: چنان دان is an imperative phrase 'consider/know thus' (چنان = 'thus', دان = 'consider/know'); که introduces the content clause that follows. دادست is a poetic contraction of داد است ('there is justice' or 'justice is'), followed by و (and) linking the contrasting noun بیداد (injustice/oppression) which is negated by نیست ('does not exist').
segments.s136-g18: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. داد is the noun 'justice' and آمدش is آمد + clitic -ش 'came to him/it' (past). جای فریاد نیست is a verbless existential statement 'there is no place for outcry' (جای = place, فریاد = outcry/cry, نیست = 'is not').
segments.s136-g19: جوانی و پیری is a coordinated nominal phrase 'youth and old age'. به نزدیک مرگ is a prepositional/adverbial phrase 'near to death' (به + نزدیک + مرگ) describing their nearness to death.
segments.s136-g20: یکی دان is an instruction 'consider (them) one' — یکی ('one') with دان ('consider/know') in an imperative sense. چو introduces the temporal clause 'when'; اندر بدن نیست برگ is an archaic word order for 'there is no leaf in the body' (اندر = in, بدن = body, نیست = is not, برگ = leaf) — a metaphorical way of saying the body is lifeless.
segments.s136-g21: This is a conditional descriptive clause: دل از نور ... آگنده‌ای = 'the heart is filled with the light ...'. از marks the source/means ('with'/'of'), نور = 'light', and آگنده‌ای is an archaic adjectival/copiular form meaning 'is filled' (آگنده + a copular ending). ایمان‌گر here functions to indicate connection to faith/being faithful (an older/poetic formation).
segments.s136-g22: ترا is the clitic sequence (ت + را) marking 'to/for you' or topical/emphatic address; خامشی = 'silence'. The pair به که is an archaic interrogative/causal phrase meaning 'why' or 'for what reason' here, introducing the question 'why keep silent?'. The final تو بنده‌ای is a simple copular clause 'you are a servant' (بنده + -ای as the copula).
segments.s136-g23: برین کار = 'in/on this affair' (بر + این/این orthographic variant ین + کار). یزدان is the subject 'God' and ترا (ت + را) is the clitic 'to you/for you' marking the beneficiary; راز نیست is 'there is no secret' or 'no secret exists' — overall: 'in this matter God has no secret from you'.
segments.s136-g24: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. جانت = جان + -ت 'your soul/your life'; با = 'with' indicating association; دیو = 'demon/devil'; انباز is an obscure/archaic name or epithet (here treated as the demon's name); نیست negates the clause ('is not'). The conditional limits the first clause: 'provided your soul is not with the demon Anbâz.'

sentences.s136-s13: Strive in this world as you pass through it; in the end you will bring goodness upon yourself.
sentences.s136-s14: Now I will first relate the battle of Sohrab, that quarrel by which he contended with his father.
sentences.s136-s5: Until everyone had climbed the gateway of greed, this secret door did not open to anyone.
sentences.s136-s6: Perhaps it is better to depart; a better place will come to him when he finds rest in another abode.
sentences.s136-s7: The moment (breath) of death is like a terrifying fire; it fears neither the young nor the decrepit.
sentences.s136-s8: In this place there is no room for lingering in going; if Death the destroyer draws tight upon the steed...
sentences.s136-s1: If a gale should come forth from the nook, it will cast the unripe bitter-orange to the ground.
sentences.s136-s2: We call him an oppressor; if (he be) just. We deem him skillful; if (he be) unskilled.
sentences.s136-s3: If death is justice, what is injustice? If (all this) stems from justice, what are all these cries and laments?
sentences.s136-s4: Your soul is unaware of this secret; there is no way for you through this veil.
sentences.s136-s9: Know this: there is justice and no injustice; when justice comes there is no place for outcry.
sentences.s136-s10: Count youth and old age alike as near death; they are one when the body has no leaf.
sentences.s136-s11: If the heart is filled with the light of faith, why keep silent? You are a servant.
sentences.s136-s12: In this matter God has no secret from you, if your soul is not with the demon Anbâz.
